name: coreweave
description: A Pulumi package for creating and managing CoreWeave cloud resources.
attribution: This Pulumi package is based on the [`coreweave` Terraform Provider](https://github.com/terraform-providers/terraform-provider-coreweave).

nodejs.packageDescription: A Pulumi package for creating and managing CoreWeave cloud resources.
nodejs.readme:
> This provider is a derived work of the [Terraform Provider](https://github.com/terraform-providers/terraform-provider-coreweave)
> distributed under [MPL 2.0](https://www.mozilla.org/en-US/MPL/2.0/). If you encounter a bug or missing feature,
> first check the [`pulumi-coreweave` repo](https://github.com/pulumi/pulumi-coreweave/issues); however, if that doesn't turn up anything,
> please consult the source [`terraform-provider-coreweave` repo](https://github.com/terraform-providers/terraform-provider-coreweave/issues).

python.readme:
> This provider is a derived work of the [Terraform Provider](https://github.com/terraform-providers/terraform-provider-coreweave)
> distributed under [MPL 2.0](https://www.mozilla.org/en-US/MPL/2.0/). If you encounter a bug or missing feature,
> first check the [`pulumi-coreweave` repo](https://github.com/pulumi/pulumi-coreweave/issues); however, if that doesn't turn up anything,
> please consult the source [`terraform-provider-coreweave` repo](https://github.com/terraform-providers/terraform-provider-coreweave/issues).

endpoint.description: CoreWeave API Endpoint. This can also be set via the COREWEAVE_API_ENDPOINT environment variable, which takes precedence. Defaults to `https://api.coreweave.com/`

httpTimeout.description: Timeout duration for the HTTP client to use. This can also be set via the COREWEAVE_HTTP_TIMEOUT environment variable, which takes precedence. If unset, defaults to 10 seconds

s3Endpoint.description: CoreWeave S3 Endpoint, used for CoreWeave Object Storage. This can also be set via the COREWEAVE_S3_ENDPOINT environment variable, which takes precedence. Defaults to `https://cwobject.com`

token.description: CoreWeave API Token in the form `CW-SECRET-<secret>`. This can also be set via the COREWEAVE_API_TOKEN environment variable, which takes precedence.

ca.description: The CA certificate for the webhook server. Must be a base64-encoded PEM-encoded certificate.

server.description: The URL of the webhook server.

ca.description: The CA certificate for the webhook server. Must be a base64-encoded PEM-encoded certificate.

server.description: The URL of the webhook server.

adminGroupBinding.description: The OIDC group that is bound to the cluster-admin role for bootstrap access to the cluster.

ca.description: The CA certificate for the OIDC issuer. Must be a base64-encoded PEM-encoded certificate.

clientId.description: The client ID for the OIDC client.

groupsClaim.description: The claim to use as the groups.

groupsPrefix.description: The prefix to use for the groups.

issuerUrl.description: The URL of the OIDC issuer.

requiredClaim.description: The claim to require for authentication.

signingAlgs.description: A list of signing algorithms that the OpenID Connect discovery endpoint uses.

usernameClaim.description: The claim to use as the username.

usernamePrefix.description: The prefix to use for the username.

dns.description: Settings affecting DNS for DHCP within the VPC

servers.description: The DNS servers to be used by DHCP clients within the VPC.

disablePublicAccess.description: Specifies whether the VPC should be blocked from consuming public Internet.

ipam.description: The configuration for a secondary host prefix.

name.description: The user-specified name of the host prefix.

prefixes.description: The VPC-wide aggregates from which host-specific prefixes are allocated. May be IPv4 or IPv6.

type.description: Controls network connectivity from the prefix to the host. Must be one of: `PRIMARY`, `ROUTED`, `ATTACHED`.

gatewayAddressPolicy.description: Describes which IP address from the prefix is allocated to the network gateway. Must be one of: `UNSPECIFIED`, `EUI64`, `FIRST_IP`, `LAST_IP`.

prefixLength.description: The desired length for each Node's allocation from the VPC-wide aggregate prefix.

disablePublicServices.description: Specifies whether the VPC should prevent public prefixes advertised from Nodes from being imported into public-facing networks, making them inaccessible from the Internet.

prefix.description: Object key prefix to which the rule applies

daysAfterInitiation.description: Days after initiation to abort multipart uploads

days.description: Number of days after object creation for expiration

expiredObjectDeleteMarker.description: Whether to remove expired delete markers

and.description: Configuration block used to apply a logical AND to two or more predicates. The Lifecycle Rule will apply to any object matching all the predicates configured inside the and block.

objectSizeGreaterThan.description: Minimum object size (in bytes) to which the rule applies.

objectSizeLessThan.description: Maximum object size (in bytes) to which the rule applies.

objectSizeGreaterThan.description: Minimum object size (in bytes) to which the rule applies.

objectSizeLessThan.description: Maximum object size (in bytes) to which the rule applies.

prefix.description: Prefix identifying one or more objects to which the rule applies.

tags.description: Map for specifying tag keys and values.

newerNoncurrentVersions.description: Number of noncurrent versions to retain

noncurrentDays.description: Days after becoming noncurrent before deletion

newerNoncurrentVersions.description: Number of noncurrent versions to retain

noncurrentDays.description: Number of days after object becomes noncurrent before the transition may occur

storageClass.description: Storage class to transition noncurrent objects to

days.description: Number of days after object creation for transition

storageClass.description: Storage class to transition objects to

status.description: Versioning state of the bucket. Valid values: Enabled, Suspended, or Disabled. Disabled should only be used when creating or importing resources that correspond to unversioned S3 buckets since the S3 API does not allow setting an Enabled/Suspended bucket to Disabled.

actions.description: Defines which operations the policy allows or denies. Organization access policies can include actions from two APIs - S3 (s3:*) and AI Object Storage API (cwobject:*). You can use wildcards (like s3:* or cwobject:*) to cover multiple actions at once.

effect.description: Must be either Allow or Deny (case-sensitive). Determines whether the statement grants or denies the specified actions on the listed resources for the designated principals. By default, all access is denied.

name.description: A short, human-readable identifier for this specific policy statement, similar to Sid in bucket access policies.

principals.description: Defines which users, roles, or groups the policy applies to. Only short-form identifiers are supported. If you use a full ARN, the policy will fail with an error. See the [AI Object Storage documentation](https://docs.coreweave.com/products/storage/object-storage/concepts/policies/organization-policies#resources) for guidelines on defining principals.

resources.description: Defines which resources the policy applies to. See the [AI Object Storage documentation](https://docs.coreweave.com/products/storage/object-storage/concepts/policies/organization-policies#resources) for guidelines on defining resources.

ca.description: The CA certificate of the authentication webhook.

server.description: The server URL of the authentication webhook.

ca.description: The CA certificate of the authorization webhook.

server.description: The server URL of the authorization webhook.

clientId.description: The client ID of the OIDC configuration.

issuerUrl.description: The issuer URL of the OIDC configuration.

dns.description: Settings affecting DNS for DHCP within the VPC

servers.description: The DNS servers advertised to DHCP clients within the VPC.

disablePublicAccess.description: True if the VPC is blocked from consuming public Internet. False otherwise.

ipam.description: The configuration for a secondary host prefix.

name.description: The user-specified name of the host prefix.

prefixes.description: The VPC-wide aggregates from which host-specific prefixes are allocated. May be IPv4 or IPv6.

type.description: Controls network connectivity from the prefix to the host.

gatewayAddressPolicy.description: Describes which IP address from the prefix is allocated to the network gateway.

prefixLength.description: The desired length for each Node's allocation from the VPC-wide aggregate prefix.

disablePublicServices.description: True if the VPC will prevent public prefixes advertised from Nodes from being imported into public-facing networks, making them inaccessible from the Internet. False otherwise.

condition.description: Map of condition operators to JSON expressions

principal.description: Map of principal types to ARNs

provider.description:
The provider type for the coreweave package. By default, resources use package-wide configuration
settings, however an explicit `Provider` instance may be created and passed during resource
construction to achieve fine-grained programmatic control over provider settings. See the
[documentation](https://www.pulumi.com/docs/reference/programming-model/#providers) for more information.

endpoint.description: CoreWeave API Endpoint. This can also be set via the COREWEAVE_API_ENDPOINT environment variable, which takes precedence. Defaults to `https://api.coreweave.com/`

httpTimeout.description: Timeout duration for the HTTP client to use. This can also be set via the COREWEAVE_HTTP_TIMEOUT environment variable, which takes precedence. If unset, defaults to 10 seconds

s3Endpoint.description: CoreWeave S3 Endpoint, used for CoreWeave Object Storage. This can also be set via the COREWEAVE_S3_ENDPOINT environment variable, which takes precedence. Defaults to `https://cwobject.com`

token.description: CoreWeave API Token in the form `CW-SECRET-<secret>`. This can also be set via the COREWEAVE_API_TOKEN environment variable, which takes precedence.

endpoint.description: CoreWeave API Endpoint. This can also be set via the COREWEAVE_API_ENDPOINT environment variable, which takes precedence. Defaults to `https://api.coreweave.com/`

httpTimeout.description: Timeout duration for the HTTP client to use. This can also be set via the COREWEAVE_HTTP_TIMEOUT environment variable, which takes precedence. If unset, defaults to 10 seconds

s3Endpoint.description: CoreWeave S3 Endpoint, used for CoreWeave Object Storage. This can also be set via the COREWEAVE_S3_ENDPOINT environment variable, which takes precedence. Defaults to `https://cwobject.com`

token.description: CoreWeave API Token in the form `CW-SECRET-<secret>`. This can also be set via the COREWEAVE_API_TOKEN environment variable, which takes precedence.

additionalServerSans.description: Additional Subject Alternative Names (SANs) to include in the Kubernetes API server TLS certificate. Maximum 10 entries.

apiServerEndpoint.description: The endpoint for the cluster's api-server.

auditPolicy.description: Audit policy for the cluster. Must be provided as a base64-encoded JSON/YAML string.

authnWebhook.description: Authentication webhook configuration for the cluster.

authzWebhook.description: Authorization webhook configuration for the cluster.

internalLbCidrNames.description:
The names of the vpc prefixes to use as internal load balancer CIDR ranges. Internal load balancers are reachable within the VPC but not accessible from the internet.
The prefixes must exist in the cluster's VPC. This field is append-only.

internalLbCidrNamesV6s.description: IPv6 Internal Load Balancer CIDR names. If any IPv6 field is set, then ALL IPv6 fields must be set.

name.description: The name of the cluster. Must not be longer than 30 characters.

nodePortRange.description: Kubernetes Service NodePort range. NodePort range can be expanded in existing clusters but not shrunk. Updating the NodePort range to a smaller range will require a replacement of the cluster.

oidc.description: OpenID Connect (OIDC) configuration for authentication to the api-server.

podCidrName.description: The name of the vpc prefix to use as the pod CIDR range. The prefix must exist in the cluster's VPC.

podCidrNameV6.description: IPv6 Pod CIDR name. If any IPv6 field is set, then ALL IPv6 fields must be set.

public.description: Whether the cluster's api-server is publicly accessible from the internet.

serviceAccountOidcIssuerUrl.description: The URL of the OIDC issuer for the cluster's service account tokens. This value corresponds to the `--service-account-issuer` flag on the kube-apiserver.

serviceCidrName.description: The name of the vpc prefix to use as the service CIDR range. The prefix must exist in the cluster's VPC.

serviceCidrNameV6.description: IPv6 Service CIDR name. If any IPv6 field is set, then ALL IPv6 fields must be set.

sharedStorageClusterId.description: The `clusterId` of the cluster to share storage with. Must be enabled by CoreWeave suppport. Contact CoreWeave support if you are interested in this feature.

status.description: The current status of the cluster.

version.description: The version of Kubernetes to run on the cluster, in minor version format (e.g. 'v1.35'). Patch versions are automatically applied by CKS as they are released.

vpcId.description: The ID of the VPC in which the cluster is located. Must be a VPC in the same Availability Zone as the cluster.

zone.description: The Availability Zone in which the cluster is located.

additionalServerSans.description: Additional Subject Alternative Names (SANs) to include in the Kubernetes API server TLS certificate. Maximum 10 entries.

auditPolicy.description: Audit policy for the cluster. Must be provided as a base64-encoded JSON/YAML string.

authnWebhook.description: Authentication webhook configuration for the cluster.

authzWebhook.description: Authorization webhook configuration for the cluster.

dhcp.description: Settings affecting DHCP behavior within the VPC.

egress.description: Settings affecting traffic leaving the VPC.

hostPrefix.description:
An IPv4 CIDR range used to allocate host addresses when booting compute into a VPC.
This CIDR must be have a mask size of /18. If left unspecified, a Zone-specific default value will be applied by the server.
This field is immutable once set.
hostPrefix.deprecationMessage: `hostPrefix` is deprecated. Use `hostPrefixes` instead. The field will be removed in a future version. The equivalent expression for a given resource may be found by refreshing state and running `terraform state show coreweave_networking_vpc.example`.

hostPrefixes.description: The IPv4 or IPv6 CIDR ranges used to allocate host addresses when booting compute into a VPC.

ingress.description: Settings affecting traffic entering the VPC.

name.description: The name of the VPC. Must not be longer than 30 characters.

vpcPrefixes.description: A list of additional prefixes associated with the VPC. For example, CKS clusters use these prefixes for Pod and service CIDR ranges.

zone.description: The Availability Zone in which the VPC is located.

pulumi:providers:coreweave/terraformConf.description: This function returns a Terraform config object with terraform-namecased keys,to be used with the Terraform Module Provider.
